description: Andrzej Sapkowski's dark fantasy saga about Geralt of Rivia, a mutant monster-hunter for hire navigating a morally grey world of humans, monsters, and politics. Born as Polish short stories in 1986, the franchise exploded globally through CD Projekt Red's acclaimed RPG trilogy — particularly The Witcher 3 (2015), one of the greatest games ever made.

media.title: The Last Wish (short story collection)
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: The foundational short story collection. Introduces Geralt, Yennefer, Dandelion. First English edition 2007. Stories include The Witcher, A Grain of Truth, The Lesser Evil, A Matter of Price, and The Last Wish.
media.notes: Start here. The Netflix S1 adapts most of this.

media.title: Sword of Destiny (short story collection)
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: Second short story collection. Introduces Ciri. Published before The Last Wish but set later. First English edition 2015.
media.notes: Read after The Last Wish. Introduces the Child Surprise / Ciri storyline.

media.title: Blood of Elves
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: First novel in the Witcher Saga. Geralt and Ciri travel together as the world descends into war. Sets up the full saga.
media.notes: Novel 1 of 5 in the Saga.

media.title: Time of Contempt
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: The Nilfgaard invasion escalates. Ciri separated from Geralt. Major political and magical upheaval.
media.notes: Novel 2 of 5.

media.title: Baptism of Fire
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: Geralt assembles a Hansa and marches south to find Ciri. Road-trip saga across a war-torn Continent.
media.notes: Novel 3 of 5.

media.title: The Tower of the Swallow
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: Ciri on the run; Geralt closing in. The saga's emotional peak.
media.notes: Novel 4 of 5.

media.title: The Lady of the Lake
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: The Saga conclusion. Ciri's destiny fulfilled. CDPR's games pick up roughly five years after this ending.
media.notes: Novel 5 of 5. End of the original Geralt saga.

media.title: Season of Storms
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: Standalone prequel novel — Geralt's swords are stolen. Set chronologically before The Last Wish events but written 14 years later. The Witcher 3 Blood and Wine draws from it.
media.notes: Standalone — read anytime after The Last Wish.

media.title: Crossroads of Ravens
media.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
media.description: Newest prequel novel (2024). Set before Blood of Elves. CDPR has hinted at incorporating elements into The Witcher 4.
media.notes: Newest Sapkowski Witcher book. International release late 2025.

media.title: The Hexer (Wiedźmin) — Film
media.description: Polish theatrical film compiled from unreleased TV series footage. Directed by Marek Brodzki. Michał Żebrowski as Geralt. Famously incoherent — a 2-hour edit of what was meant to be 13 episodes. Polish-only release.
media.notes: Non-canon to CDPR or Netflix. Sapkowski himself criticized it. Historical curiosity only.

media.title: The Hexer (Wiedźmin) — TV Series
media.description: Polish TV series. 13 episodes. More coherent than the film, but still critically panned and canceled after one season. Covers Geralt's childhood through early adventures. Never officially released internationally.
media.notes: Polish-language only. No international distribution. Non-canon to games/Netflix. Historical curiosity.

media.title: The Witcher (Metropolis Software) — CANCELED
media.description: A Witcher game was in development at Metropolis Software from 1996–1997 before being abandoned. The game's director Adrian Chmielarz actually coined the English word 'witcher' during this project. Never released.
media.notes: CANCELED. Only historically relevant — this is where the word 'witcher' comes from in English.

media.title: The Witcher
media.description: Geralt awakes with amnesia in a world rebuilding after the Nilfgaard wars. He hunts Salamandra, a criminal syndicate that stole witcher secrets. Unconventional combo-click combat. Enhanced Edition (2008) is the version to play.
media.notes: PC only (GoG). Enhanced Edition recommended. ~40-50 hours. CDPR's first game.

media.title: The Witcher: Rise of the White Wolf — CANCELED
media.description: Planned console port of The Witcher 1 with a rebuilt engine and new combat system. Canceled in spring 2009 due to publisher issues with Atari. Never released.
media.notes: CANCELED. Would have been the first console Witcher game.

media.title: The Witcher: Crimson Trail
media.description: Side-scrolling hack-and-slash mobile game for Java feature phones, released alongside the original Witcher PC game. Young Geralt hunting monsters. Considers a prequel. Abandonware now — the official site no longer exists.
media.notes: DEFUNCT. J2ME feature phone era. Can be found on abandonware sites. Not playable on modern smartphones.

media.title: The Witcher: Versus
media.description: Flash-based browser PvP fighting game. Three character classes: Witcher, Sorceress, Frightener. Tied to the thewitcher.com forum accounts. Shut down when Flash died.
media.notes: DEFUNCT. Flash-based. Dead as of Flash EOL (2020). Tie-in to the original game's community.

media.title: The Witcher 2: Assassins of Kings
media.description: Geralt is framed for regicide and must clear his name while navigating a brutal war of succession. Entirely different Chapter 2 depending on a single timed decision. Stunning for its era. Enhanced Edition (2012) on PC; Xbox 360 version (2012) requires streaming via Sunshine.
media.notes: PC or Xbox 360. Chapter 2 branches completely based on one choice in Ch.1. ~30-40 hours.

media.title: The Witcher Adventure Game
media.description: Digital adaptation of the physical board game. Up to 4 players as Geralt, Triss, Yarpen, or Dandelion. Quest-based adventure game. Available on iOS and Android.
media.notes: Still available on mobile. Casual — good for series fans, not required.

media.title: The Witcher Battle Arena
media.description: Free-to-play MOBA for mobile. 6-player capture-and-hold battles with 8 Witcher-universe heroes. Shut down December 31, 2015 after just over a year — servers closed permanently.
media.notes: DEFUNCT. Servers shut Dec 31 2015. MOBA trend chaser that didn't survive.

media.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
media.description: Geralt searches for Ciri as the Wild Hunt closes in. One of the greatest RPGs ever made. 100+ hours with both expansions. Next-Gen Edition (2022) is the version to play. Switch port playable but visually degraded.
media.notes: Play this. Next-Gen Edition on PC/PS5 recommended. Complete Edition includes Hearts of Stone + Blood and Wine DLCs.

media.title: Hearts of Stone (DLC)
media.description: Witcher 3 expansion. Geralt bound in a cursed contract with an immortal trickster, Gaunter O'Dimm. ~10-15 hours. Often considered the best Witcher story ever written.
media.notes: Requires TW3 base game. Play after the main story or mid-playthrough.

media.title: Blood and Wine (DLC)
media.description: Witcher 3 expansion. Toussaint — a sun-drenched vineyard land hiding a monster of legend. Geralt's final chapter. Enormous — 20+ hours, essentially a full game. Perfect ending for the Geralt era.
media.notes: Play last. Geralt's send-off. Draws heavily from Season of Storms novel.

media.title: GWENT: The Witcher Card Game
media.description: Standalone version of the card game from Witcher 3. Free-to-play digital CCG. PC and mobile. Still live as of 2026. The mobile version launched in 2019.
media.notes: Free-to-play. Still active on iOS/Android and GOG/Steam.

media.title: Thronebreaker: The Witcher Tales
media.description: Single-player RPG using GWENT mechanics. Queen Meve of Lyria faces Nilfgaard invasion. 30-hour story that serves as a GWENT prequel. Mobile version released 2020-2021.
media.notes: Available on iOS and Android. Premium paid title — no F2P. Standalone story, no TW3 knowledge needed.

media.title: The Witcher: Nightmare of the Wolf
media.description: Netflix animated film. Origin story of Vesemir — the oldest witcher of Kaer Morhen. Covers the catastrophe that wiped out most witchers. Studio Mir animation (same as Avatar: The Legend of Korra). Canon to Netflix continuity.
media.notes: Netflix. Watch before TW series Season 2 for maximum impact. Voiced by Theo James (Vesemir).

media.title: The Witcher: Monster Slayer
media.description: Augmented-reality location-based RPG (Pokémon GO-style). Set before Geralt's era — players become new witchers hunting monsters in the real world. Reached 500k downloads in 3 days; over 100 million monsters slain before shutdown. Shut down June 30, 2023.
media.notes: DEFUNCT. Servers shut June 30 2023. AR game for iOS/Android — no longer downloadable.

media.title: The Witcher: Roach Race
media.description: Free mobile endless runner / platformer. Play as Roach, Geralt's horse. Five levels. Also embedded as an arcade minigame in Cyberpunk 2077 Patch 1.6. Silly fan-service spinoff.
media.notes: Still available free on iOS/Android. Lighthearted. Also playable in Cyberpunk 2077.

media.title: The Witcher (Netflix) — Season 1
media.description: Henry Cavill as Geralt. Adapts The Last Wish and Sword of Destiny short stories across three non-linear timelines (Geralt/Yennefer/Ciri). Famously confusing on first watch — the timelines converge at episode 6.
media.notes: Netflix. Season 1 uses non-linear storytelling — all three arcs are in different time periods simultaneously.

media.title: The Witcher (Netflix) — Season 2
media.description: Linear timeline. Geralt brings Ciri to Kaer Morhen. Adapts loosely from Blood of Elves. Received much better than Season 1 for coherent structure.
media.notes: Netflix. Watch Nightmare of the Wolf before this for full Vesemir/Kaer Morhen context.

media.title: The Witcher: Blood Origin
media.description: Netflix limited series. 1200 years before Geralt. Origin of the first Witcher, the Conjunction of Spheres, and the first elven mages. Mixed reception; considered the weakest Netflix Witcher entry.
media.notes: Netflix. 4 episodes. Chronologically first in the Netflix universe. Optional — standalone world-building.

media.title: The Witcher (Netflix) — Season 3
media.description: Henry Cavill's final season as Geralt. Adapts Time of Contempt. Released in two parts (Vol.1: June, Vol.2: July 2023). Cavill announced departure mid-season rollout, creating a bittersweet finale.
media.notes: Netflix. Henry Cavill's last season. Split release — 5 episodes then 3.

media.title: The Witcher: Sirens of the Deep
media.description: Netflix animated film. Adapts 'A Little Sacrifice' from the Sword of Destiny collection — Geralt hired to investigate attacks on a coastal village by sea people. Doug Cockle (TW game series) voices Geralt. Animation by Kang Hei Chul.
media.notes: Netflix. Doug Cockle voices Geralt — same voice actor as the games. Set in the Netflix continuity.

media.title: The Witcher (Netflix) — Season 4
media.description: Liam Hemsworth replaces Henry Cavill as Geralt. Mixed reception largely driven by the recasting controversy. Continues the main Continent storyline.
media.notes: Netflix. Liam Hemsworth era begins. Cavill confirmed it was his choice to leave for Superman.

media.title: The Rats: A Witcher Tale
media.description: Netflix animated spin-off film following The Rats — the gang of young outlaws Ciri runs with in the books. Quietly dropped on Netflix. Based on events from Tower of the Swallow.
media.notes: Netflix. Released quietly alongside Season 4 build-up.

media.title: The Witcher (Netflix) — Season 5 (UPCOMING)
media.description: Confirmed final season of the Netflix Witcher series. Liam Hemsworth as Geralt. Will conclude the Netflix storyline.
media.notes: UPCOMING. Netflix has confirmed Season 5 will be the last.

media.title: The Witcher 4 / Project Polaris (UPCOMING)
media.description: The Witcher IV — first in a new trilogy. Ciri as protagonist. Revealed at The Game Awards 2024 with cinematic trailer. Built on Unreal Engine 5. CDPR confirmed it will not launch before 2027.
media.notes: UPCOMING. No release date confirmed. Not before 2027 per CDPR earnings call March 2025.

media.title: Project Sirius (UPCOMING — Spinoff)
media.developer: The Molasses Flood (absorbed by CDPR April 2025)
media.description: Co-op/multiplayer Witcher spinoff with single-player campaign. Rebooted in early 2023. Developed by The Molasses Flood, now fully absorbed into CDPR. No release window known.
media.notes: UPCOMING. Development rebooted 2023. The Molasses Flood merged into CDPR April 2025.

media.title: Canis Majoris / Witcher 1 Remake (UPCOMING)
media.description: Full remake of The Witcher (2007) in Unreal Engine 5, developed by Polish indie studio Fool's Theory under CDPR supervision. Still in concept phase as of early 2025.
media.notes: UPCOMING. Still early concept phase. Fool's Theory has Witcher 3 and BG3 veterans.

media.title: The Witcher: House of Glass (Comic)
media.description: Dark Horse Comics. Original story by Paul Tobin. Geralt investigates a haunted manor in the forest. Set in the game continuity.
media.notes: Dark Horse. Game-universe canon. First of several Dark Horse arcs.

media.title: The Witcher: Fox Children (Comic)
media.description: Dark Horse Comics. Adapts a chapter from Season of Storms. Geralt chases a mysterious creature through a swamp.
media.notes: Dark Horse. Adapts Season of Storms material.

media.title: The Witcher: Curse of Crows (Comic)
media.description: Dark Horse Comics. Original story continuing from The Witcher 3. Ciri and Geralt face a vengeful witch.
media.notes: Dark Horse. Set after TW3.

media.title: The Witcher: Of Flesh and Flame (Comic)
media.description: Dark Horse Comics. Geralt escorted on a desert caravan, uncovering a conspiracy. Original story.
media.notes: Dark Horse. Game-universe canon.

media.title: The Witcher: Ronin (Manga)
media.description: Crowdfunded manga (Kickstarter, Sept 2021). Geralt in Feudal Japan fighting yokai instead of European monsters. Illustrated in manga style. Ships started in March 2022.
media.notes: Non-canon alternate-universe. Fun what-if. CDPR-endorsed.

media.title: The Witcher: Wiedźmin (Polish Comics, 1993–1995)
media.description: Original Polish comic book series by Bogusław Polch (art) and Maciej Parowski (script). Released before the games, adapted short stories from The Last Wish. Available in Polish only; partial English fan-translations exist.
media.notes: Polish only. Based on Sapkowski's short stories. Historically significant as the first Witcher visual media.
